dressCode.defaultDisclaimer: By tradition, white (or similar shades) is reserved for the bride.

calendar.heading: Calendars
calendar.singleDescription: We would love to celebrate with you.
calendar.eventDescription: We will be waiting for you at {{venue}}.
calendar.noEvents: There are no enabled events to add to the calendar.

footer.thanks: Thank you for celebrating with us!

language.label: Site language
language.hint: This language is saved in site.language and defines the final site language.

header.title: Wedding Config Builder
header.subtitle: Configure your wedding invitation in minutes

alerts.enableOneEvent: You must enable at least one event (religious, civil or party) to continue.

2.label: Step 2 of 9
2.title: Welcome screen
2.desc: The first screen guests see when opening your invitation.

3.label: Step 3 of 9
3.title: Ceremonies and party
3.desc: Event details. You can enable or disable each one.

4.label: Step 4 of 9
4.title: Our Story
4.desc: Tell the story with text and up to 4 photos.

7.label: Step 7 of 9
7.title: Extras
7.desc: Social media, playlist and useful info.

8.label: Step 8 of 9
8.title: Share image
8.desc: Set how your invitation link looks when shared.

9.label: Step 9 of 9
9.title: Done! Your configuration
9.desc: Download the file and follow the instructions.

labels.themeVisualDesc: Choose the overall invitation style. It only changes visual style: colors, fonts and ornaments.

hints.displayName: This will appear in the hero section and footer.
hints.heroBackgroundImage: Upload this file to public/media/hero/ in your repository.
hints.eventTimezone: One option per UTC offset with a representative city.
hints.eventsEnabled: You must enable at least one event to continue. Calendar links will include 1, 2 or 3 events based on your selection.
hints.storyPhotos: The first photo is required. Select your files to define names, then upload them to public/media/couple/ using those same names.
hints.usefulInfoCards: Search icons in Lucide Icons and use names like lucide:camera.
hints.pageTitle: This is also the browser tab title.
hints.ogImage: Upload this file to public/media/og/. Recommended size: 1200x630.

instructions.title: Instructions to publish your invitation
instructions.item1: If you still do not have a repo, go to the template repository and click "Use this template" -> "Create a new repository".
instructions.item2: In your new repo, open Settings -> Pages and choose "GitHub Actions" as source.
instructions.item3: Open src/data/ -> click wedding.yml -> click the pencil -> select all -> paste your YAML -> "Commit changes".
instructions.item4:
Upload your photos to the matching folders in GitHub:
  • Hero image: upload to public/media/hero/
  • Story photos: upload to public/media/couple/
  • Social share image: upload to public/media/og/
instructions.item5: Done! GitHub Actions will build and publish your site in a few minutes at https://YOUR_USER.github.io/YOUR_REPO/.

site.metaDescription: You are invited to celebrate this special moment with us.

story.heading: Our story
story.sentence: Five years ago we met on that trip and knew we wanted to walk together forever.

socials.heading: Social Media
socials.body: Before the wedding, you will receive a link to upload all your photos and videos.

gifts.heading: Gifts
gifts.body: Your presence is the most important thing, but if you would like to give us a gift you can help us with our honeymoon.

dressCode.title: Dress Code
dressCode.body: We are waiting for your best look to celebrate together.
dressCode.brideDisclaimer: By tradition, white or similar shades are reserved for the bride.

rsvp.text: Please confirm your attendance before May 1.

playlist.heading: Let's build the playlist together
playlist.body: Add those songs that cannot be missing from the party.
